name: Break-Even Analysis
summary: Calculate the point at which revenue covers all costs. Tells you how many customers or how much revenue you need to stop losing money.
whenToUseIt: When launching, setting prices, or deciding whether a new product or market is viable.
steps: List all fixed costs (rent, salaries, subscriptions — costs that do not change with volume)
Calculate variable cost per unit/customer (delivery, support, hosting per customer)
Calculate contribution margin per unit (price per unit - variable cost per unit)
Break-even point = fixed costs / contribution margin per unit
example: Fixed costs: $20K/month. Price: $200/month per customer. Variable cost: $50/month per customer. Contribution margin: $150. Break-even: $20K / $150 = 134 customers needed to break even.
